Ad Text:
Senior Mechatronics Software Engineer – VIC
Full-time/Permanent
Location: Port Melbourne
Salary Range: $120K to $160K including super
Seeking a highly skilled Senior Mechatronics Software Engineer with strong problem-solving skills and expertise in embedded systems, IoT, control systems, Python/C++, ROS, leading data-driven engineering to lead intelligent, next-generation teams.
Applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents currently residing in Australia.
 
About the Role 

Develop the intelligence powering smart fleet vehicles to capture and analyze real-world data.
Lead, innovate, integrate, and accelerate with a team driving data-led engineering and transforming real-world challenges into impactful solutions.
 
Key responsibilities of the role
In this role, you will serve as a key point of contact, guiding technical direction and ensuring successful delivery outcomes for the team.

Problem Solving: Proven capability in problem solving and critical thinking, including breaking down technical challenges, evaluating options & recommending the best outcome forward
Software & Systems Development: Design, develop, and maintain embedded software and firmware for smart devices, automation systems, and connected mechatronic platforms.
Data Integration: Implement data collection, logging, and communication between hardware components and cloud or edge systems to enable analysis and optimisation.
Control Systems Engineering: Develop and tune advanced control algorithms (e.g. PID, model predictive, adaptive control) for sensors, actuators, and robotic systems.
Connectivity & IoT: Integrate communication protocols such as CAN, Modbus, and TCP/IP to support real-time data exchange in networked systems.
Simulation & Testing: Model and simulate mechatronic systems using tools such as MATLAB/Simulink, LabVIEW, or Python, and perform hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for validation.
Collaboration & Data Utilisation: Partner with data engineers and scientists to feed operational data into analytics or machine learning pipelines, supporting predictive maintenance and system optimisation.
Documentation & Compliance: Prepare detailed documentation of system architecture, software design, and data flow processes while ensuring alignment with organisational standards, safety, and quality requirements.
 
Essential Skills and Experience

Relevant tertiary qualification in Mechatronics, Software Engineering, or related discipline
5-10 years of relevant experience in mechatronic software and systems engineering
Proven experience applying critical thinking and problem-solving to a range of technical challenges
Experience with leading, supervising or mentoring junior engineers/ teams previously
Proficiency in Python, C++, SQL, and firmware development.
Experience with ROS (Robot Operating System), PLC programming, and industrial automation frameworks.
Knowledge of machine learning / AI integration in mechatronic systems, including image recognition.
Familiarity with digital twins, predictive analytics, and data visualisation.
Understanding of cybersecurity principles for connected devices.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a focus on system reliability and data quality.
Excellent communication, teamwork, and documentation capabilities.
 
Preferred Skills:
Smart manufacturing (Industry 4.0), autonomous robotics, IoT devices, data-driven maintenance, digital twins, and safety/embedded software standards.
 
Additional Info:
Occasional travel to clients, interstate offices, and events required.

Keyword Search
Senior Mechatronics Engineer, Senior Mechatronics Software Engineer, Robotics Software Engineer, Control Systems Engineer, Systems Engineer (Mechatronics / Robotics)
 
Interested to learn more, Feel free to call me on 📱03 8080 7260 or share your resume at 📎nick.reddy@peoplebank.com.au.

I’m always happy to connect with talented professionals, referrals are welcome and appreciated.

Job Title: Databricks Senior Data Engineer (Data Engineering and AI)
Ad Text:
About the Role

Hiring for a large Australian insurer undergoing a significant cloud and data transformation, and we are looking for a Senior Databricks Solution expert to play a central role in that journey. Sitting within the Data & Analytics function, you will lead the design and delivery of the enterprise lakehouse platform — migrating legacy data infrastructure to Databricks on Azure, building production-grade data engineering pipelines, and laying the foundations for an enterprise ML and AI platform.

This is a high-impact, hands-on senior role. You will work at the intersection of data engineering, cloud architecture, and applied AI/ML - influencing both technical direction and enterprise data strategy at scale. You will collaborate closely with Engineering Leads, the solution architects, and business units across the organisation.

Key Responsibilities




Cloud Migration & Lakehouse Build

Lead the end-to-end migration of legacy on-premises and cloud data warehouses to a Databricks lakehouse on Azure

Design the target-state lakehouse architecture using Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, and the medallion pattern (Bronze / Silver / Gold)

Define migration strategies, cutover plans, and rollback procedures for enterprise-scale data estate migration

Establish Unity Catalog governance: data lineage, access controls, column-level security, and audit logging

Architect and optimise Azure infrastructure including ADLS Gen2, Azure Data Factory, Azure DevOps, and VNet integration

Data Engineering & Pipelines

Design and build scalable, production-grade data pipelines using Apache Spark (PySpark) and Databricks Workflows

Establish CI/CD standards for data — automated testing, code review processes, environment promotion, and observability

Develop reusable pipeline frameworks, ingestion patterns, and platform accelerators for use across engineering squads

Implement real-time and near-real-time streaming solutions using Apache Kafka or Azure Event Hubs with Structured Streaming

Drive performance tuning, cluster optimisation, and cost governance across the Databricks platform

Define and enforce data quality standards, SLA monitoring, and pipeline reliability practices

ML / AI Platform

Define standards for model deployment, versioning, monitoring, and retraining workflows in a production environment

Collaborate with data scientists and ML engineers to operationalise models and embed AI capabilities into business processes

Evaluate emerging Databricks AI capabilities (e.g. Mosaic AI, Databricks Assistant, DBRX) and assess fit for enterprise use cases

Architecture Leadership & Governance

Serve as the senior technical authority for the Databricks platform across the enterprise

Define and maintain architecture standards, reference architectures, ADRs, and platform runbooks

Mentor and upskill a team of data engineers through design reviews, pair programming, and knowledge-sharing sessions

Engage senior business stakeholders to communicate architecture decisions, roadmap, and risk

Required Skills & Experience

5–8 years in data engineering, data architecture, or solutions architecture roles

Minimum 3 years of hands-on Databricks experience in production enterprise environments

Deep expertise in Apache Spark (PySpark), Delta Lake, Databricks Workflows, and Databricks SQL

Demonstrated experience leading or contributing to a cloud data migration — legacy warehouse to lakehouse

Strong Azure platform skills: ADLS Gen2, Azure Data Factory, Azure DevOps, Azure Active Directory, and networking

Experience with Unity Catalog or similar enterprise data governance and cataloguing tools

Streaming experience with Apache Kafka or Azure Event Hubs and Databricks Structured Streaming

Excellent stakeholder communication — able to convey complex architecture to both engineers and executives




Apply with your CV and a short summary (no more than one page) of a cloud data migration or lakehouse project you have led or architected — what the challenge was, your approach, and the outcome. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a technical architecture discussion.

At Google, we have a vision of empowerment and equitable opportunity for all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and commit to building reconciliation through Google’s technology, platforms and people and we welcome Indigenous applicants. Please see our Reconciliation Action Plan for more information.

Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
8 years of experience with software development in one or more programming languages (e.g., Python, C, C++, Java, JavaScript).

3 years of experience in a technical leadership role; overseeing projects, with 2 years of experience in a people management, supervision/team leadership role.

Preferred qualifications:

Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science or related technical field.

3 years of experience working in a changing organization.

Experience with web-based User Interfaces (UIs) or front-end development, design patterns, and collaboration with User Experience (UX) designers and researchers will be desirable.
About the job
Like Google's own ambitions, the work of a Software Engineer goes beyond just Search. Software Engineering Managers have not only the technical expertise to take on and provide technical leadership to major projects, but also manage a team of Engineers. You not only optimize your own code but make sure Engineers are able to optimize theirs. As a Software Engineering Manager you manage your project goals, contribute to product strategy and help develop your team. Teams work all across the company, in areas such as information retrieval, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking, security, data compression, user interface design; the list goes on and is growing every day. Operating with scale and speed, our exceptional software engineers are just getting started -- and as a manager, you guide the way.

With technical and leadership expertise, you manage engineers across multiple teams and locations, a large product budget and oversee the deployment of large-scale projects across multiple sites internationally.

NetSoft is part of a global software team that writes software to manage the forecasting, planning, design and delivery of Google's Wide Area Network (WAN) network (AlphaNet), as well as aspects of our datacenters. Google's WAN network is an essential part of Google's products and services including Cloud, Youtube, Search, and Deepmind/Machine Learning (ML). The software is used by thousands of internal stakeholders to manage our data center spaces and WAN network plans, and is critical to Google's ability to build the network that our internal stakeholders and the world needs.Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.

Responsibilities

Set and communicate team priorities that support the broader organization's goals, and align strategy, processes, and decision-making across teams.
Review code developed by other engineers and provide feedback to ensure best practices (e.g., style guidelines, checking code in, accuracy, testability, and efficiency).
Drive the technical strategy, design, and roadmap for a suite of software systems, ensuring they provide an accurate, fast, and consistent user experience for network and data center planning.
Lead and drive a team of 10+ software engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence, accountability and professional growth.
Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

Lab3 is one of Australasia’s largest Azure engineering practices, specialising in Cloud & DevOps, Data, IoT & AI, Modern Workplace, Security, and Networks. We’re seeking a Principal Consultant- AI  to join our Professional Services team.  This is a senior, client-facing leadership role accountable for shaping, selling, and delivering high impact AI and Agentic AI programs for enterprise clients, working across the AI lifecycle from prototyping to production.

 

The Role:

Client Leadership & Advisory 

Act as a trusted advisor to senior client stakeholders
Lead AI strategy engagements, including use case prioritization, value articulation, and delivery roadmaps. 
Translate business strategy into scalable AI architectures and operating models. 

Solution Architecture & Technical Authority 

Provide architectural leadership across Agentic AI, GenAI, RAG, MLOps, and data platforms on Azure. 
Define reference architectures, nonfunctional requirements, and design patterns for enterprise AI systems. 
Review and approve HLD/LLD produced by delivery teams; intervene on complex or high-risk designs. 
Guide model selection, orchestration patterns, governance approaches, and platform choices aligned to business constraints. 

Delivery Oversight & Risk Management 

Own end to end technical accountability for large or complex AI programs. 
Derisk delivery through early architecture validation, proof points, and phased adoption strategies. 
Establish delivery standards across security, observability, MLOps, and responsible AI. 
Support engagement leads with estimates, delivery plans, and dependency management. 

PreSales & Commercial Contribution 

Lead or support pre-sales activities including discovery, solution shaping, estimates, SOW input, and technical assurance. 
Contribute to win-strategy through differentiated architecture, accelerators, and compelling narratives. 
Act as a technical escalation point during bids and client negotiations. 

Practice & Capability Development 

Define and evolve AI standards, accelerators, and reusable assets for LAB3. 
Mentor Senior Engineers and Consultants; uplift architectural thinking and consulting maturity. 
Contribute to hiring, role design, and skills benchmarking. 
Represent LAB3 externally through thought leadership, client showcases, and partner engagements. 

Governance, Security & Responsible AI 

Set best practice approaches for AI governance, risk, compliance, and ethics. 
Ensure solutions align with enterprise security models
Champion responsible AI principles

 

Our ideal candidate:

 

Core Technical Expertise:

Microsoft AI & Data Stack: Azure AI Foundry, Azure AI Search (vector & hybrid), Azure OpenAI, Azure ML, Fabric / Databricks. 
Agentic AI & GenAI: Agent frameworks, RAG at scale, orchestration patterns, tool/function calling, prompt optimisation. 
Architecture: Distributed systems, event driven design, API strategies, data platform integration. 
MLops & Platform Engineering: CI/CD, model lifecycle management, IaC, containers, AKS. 
Observability: End to end telemetry across application, data, and model layers. 
Security: Enterprise identity, networking, data protection, and compliance patterns. 

Consulting & Leadership Capability 

Proven experience leading large or complex client engagements. 
Strong commercial acumen: value framing, estimation, and scope control. 
Exceptional communication—credible with executives and deeply technical teams alike. 
Ability to coach, influence, and set direction without direct authority. 

Must be based anywhere in Australia, a citizen, and ideally hold security clearance

What’s in it for you?  

Be part of a team that leverages modern technologies to solve real problems and provides top level of customer satisfaction
Work with a Microsoft Partner of the Year award winner with multiple specialisations
Be supported by experienced peers and leaders that value technical expertise and encourages innovation, with clear career pathways and ongoing learning
Enjoy a supportive workplace that promotes inclusion, flexibility, diversity, and celebrates differences.
Take advantage of largely working from home in our remote/hybrid workplace and enjoy the flexibility to balance your life  
 Thrive in a community with strong values #BeTrue #TeamUp #StandOut #ThinkAhead #FearLessAchieveMore 

   

Apply now, call or connect on LinkedIn for further information.
